However, I found a plant called Vigna, but more specifically, Vigna angularis (also known as Adzuki bean or Vigna gynocrates may be referring to this, though Vigna gynocrates is not found) is a plant in the legume family. Vigna angularis has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-diabetic properties. The bioactive constituents of Vigna angularis include flavonoids, saponins, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds. The most common herbal preparations made with Vigna angularis include tea, capsules, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease.
